name: Harvest Foccacia
recipeInstructions:
Mix the warm milk, yeast, sugar, and 1/2 cup of flour in a mixing bowl. Let stand to foam for 15 minutes. While the sponge is activating, start the filling by warming the olive oil on medium heat until approximately 200°F. Add the raisins, rosemary, and grapes; then turn off the heat. Let set until room temperature.
Mix half of the filling mixture into the sponge. Gradually add four cups of flour to the mixer using the dough hook attachment. Mix until smooth. Add salt and the remaining flour one cup at a time. Knead in the machine for three minutes. The dough should be velvety and elastic. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise (approximately 1 hour).
Shaping:
Oil a cookie pan with olive oil. Roll the dough out to fit inside the pan. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise a second time until doubled in volume. Press finger indentations into the dough, making sure not to puncture all the way through. Spread the remaining topping on top of the dough. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon each of sugar and salt on top and bake at 350°F until golden brown.
